compiled_state.title: Add searchable UI/UX design knowledge base as a reference library
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Context
Agents running design-review, ui-designer, brainstorm, preplanning, and sprint workflows currently operate without a shared corpus of UI/UX design knowledge. Each session starts without government-specific anti-patterns, USWDS component guidance, WCAG 2.2 compliance patterns, or established design principles, causing inconsistent and incomplete guidance. This epic establishes the foundational reference library — schema, corpus structure, full initial content, retrieval infrastructure, and source provenance tracking — that five subsequent epics will integrate into specific agent workflows. It absorbs the government anti-pattern catalog from epic 17de-a47a, seeds the full corpus from permissively licensed sources identified during research, and establishes the retrieval contract (ref-query.sh) that all downstream integration epics depend on.

## Success Criteria
- A corpus directory at plugins/dso/data/ui-reference/ with domain-partitioned Markdown files (each ≤500 lines), YAML frontmatter per entry (id, title, domain[], component[], action[], compliance[], severity, story_type[], coverage, source, license), and three Markdown resolution tiers per entry (### Summary, ### Detail, ### Implementation). A _schema.yaml + check-corpus-schema.sh pre-commit hook enforce tag vocabulary across six dimensions: domain, component, action, compliance, severity, story_type.
- A _index.yaml manifest committed to the repository (not generated at build time), listing every entry with six-dimension tags, one-liner, file path, coverage tier, source, and license. Full inventory readable without loading any corpus file. Regenerated and recommitted on corpus changes.
- A plugins/dso/scripts/ref-query.sh retrieval script using stdlib Python BM25 (self-contained, no external deps); bm25s used as optional performance accelerator if installed, falls back with stderr notice if not. Flags: --top-n (default 8), --tier=summary|detail|implementation, --session-hash dedup, zero-result stderr signal ([ref-query: no results for query: "..." with filters: ...]), completes within 10 seconds.
- Initial corpus covers the full set identified during research: government anti-patterns (all 48 from 17de-a47a at Detail tier; Implementation stretch), USWDS component and token guidance, WCAG 2.2 AAA patterns cross-referenced to Section 508/OMB M-24-08, Nielsen's 10 usability heuristics, Don Norman's principles, GOV.UK Government Design Principles, 18F UX Guide principles, digital.gov federal requirements, Inclusive Design Principles, cognitive load and defensive design checklists, Brignull dark pattern taxonomy, SageSELab AidUI 27-class taxonomy, and representative entries from benbrignell/principles.design. Summary + Detail required for all entries; Implementation is stretch.
- Integration test at tests/integration/test-ref-library.sh (REPO_ROOT-based paths): (a) session-timeout query returns entry tagged auth/forms or containing "session timeout" in Summary; (b) --tier=summary output has ### Summary, no ### Implementation; (c) manifest integrity — every _index.yaml entry resolves to a file at its declared path; (d) no corpus file exceeds 500 lines; (e) precision sub-test — three representative queries ("USWDS form validation", "WCAG 2.2 keyboard navigation", "government authentication anti-pattern") each return a top result whose frontmatter tags match a predefined expected-tag mapping; (f) completes within 10 seconds.
- ref-query.sh registered as dso ref-query shim; check-shim-refs.sh does not block downstream skill file references.
- A docs/ui-reference-sources.yaml file at the project root tracks each external reference source: name, URL, type (github-repo|web-spec|website), pinned version/commit, last-synced date, license, and which corpus domains it populates. This is the provenance record for all corpus entries.

## Scenario Analysis
- bm25s corpus load time timeout (high, runtime): addressed by SC3 10s target and SC5f timing check
- Tag pre-filter zero matches silently (high, runtime): addressed by SC3 zero-result stderr signal and SC1 vocabulary enforcement
- Missing _index.yaml on fresh checkout (critical, deployment): addressed by SC2 committed-not-generated requirement
- Integration test paths break in worktree sessions (high, deployment): addressed by SC5 REPO_ROOT-based path resolution
- Pre-commit integrity blocks downstream skill refs before merge (high, deployment): addressed by Dependencies merge-ordering note
- shim-exempt not applied to ref-query.sh (medium, deployment): addressed by SC6 shim registration
- YAML vocabulary drift causes silent retrieval gaps (high, configuration): addressed by SC1 schema file and check-corpus-schema.sh

## Research Findings
Permissively licensed repos identified for corpus seeding: uswds/uswds (CC0), SteveBarnett/Checklists (MIT), benbrignell/principles.design (MIT), JenniKobylskiUX/WCAG-Accessibility-UX-Checklist (MIT), SageSELab/AidUI (MIT). Gap confirmed: no existing structured machine-readable government UI/UX reference catalog found — this epic fills a genuine void. Stdlib Python BM25 preferred over hard bm25s dependency at this corpus size; bm25s retained as optional accelerator.

## Dependencies
- Epic 17de-a47a: 48 anti-patterns (see ticket 17de-a47a description) copied at implementation start; closed as superseded on this epic's completion. Does not block this epic.
- No new runtime dependencies — stdlib BM25 implementation is self-contained.
- Downstream epics 2–6: Unblocked only after SC3 and SC5 pass. This epic must merge to main before downstream skill files reference plugins/dso/scripts/ref-query.sh (pre-commit referential integrity enforcement).

## Approach
Option A — Markdown files with YAML frontmatter, domain-partitioned under plugins/dso/data/ui-reference/ (e.g., anti-patterns/auth.md, anti-patterns/forms.md, styles/catalog.md, components/uswds-patterns.md). _index.yaml manifest committed to repo. ref-query.sh (stdlib BM25, optional bm25s accelerator) registered as dso ref-query shim. docs/ui-reference-sources.yaml at project root tracks external source provenance. Only script output enters agent context — corpus files never loaded directly. All five downstream integration epics call .claude/scripts/dso ref-query.

### Planning Intelligence Log
- Web research (Step 2.6): triggered — novel architectural pattern (BM25 corpus retrieval); re-triggered via gate for design tutorials and GitHub reference material research
- Bright-line conditions fired: novel architectural pattern, unfamiliar dependency (bm25s → resolved to stdlib implementation)
- Scenario analysis (Step 2.75): triggered — 8 of 14 scenarios survived blue team filter
- Practitioner-requested additional cycles: web research re-run 1 time (design tutorials + GitHub reference repos)
- Follow-on scrutiny (Step 0): not triggered — follow-ons created as placeholders at user direction
- Feasibility resolution (Step 2.5): not triggered
- LLM-instruction signal (Step 5): not triggered

comments.body: Adversarial review: 11 findings, 8 accepted. Full exchange: /tmp/workflow-plugin-8c20e0fd310e4beb/adversarial-review-0b7c-d811.json
comments.author: Claude
